terms.definition: Automatically slicing a sample into multiple parts based on transient detection. Each slice becomes a separate playable sound assigned to different pads.
Saving changes permanently by holding MAIN/COMMIT. Changes to samples, patterns, and settings must be committed to persist after power off.
A slider control assignable to different parameters per group, including volume, pitch, filter, and effects. Fader movements can be recorded into the sequencer for automation.
One of four sound banks (A, B, C, D) in a project. Each group has 12 sounds and up to 99 patterns. All groups play simultaneously for layered arrangements.
The product line name for Teenage Engineering's sampler series. The EP-133 K.O. II is the successor to the PO-33 K.O. Pocket Operator.
A sequence of notes and automation for one group. Each group can have up to 99 patterns, each 1-99 bars long. Patterns can be chained in Song Mode.
The number of sounds that can play simultaneously. The EP-133 supports 16 mono voices or 12 stereo voices thanks to OS 2.0 improvements.
Performance effects that apply momentarily while holding a pad. Release the pad to return to normal sound. OS 2.0 added pressure sensitivity for dynamic control.
Automatic alignment of recorded notes to a timing grid. Options include OFF (no quantize), 1/8, 1/16, and 1/32 note resolution.
Recording the EP-133's audio output as a new sample (OS 2.0 feature). Captures all sounds, effects, and processing into a single sample for bouncing or creative use.
A recorded audio clip stored in one of 999 slots. Samples are recorded at 46.875 kHz / 16-bit and can be mono or stereo, using the 64 MB total memory.
A snapshot of which pattern is active in each of the four groups. Scenes are used in Song Mode to create arrangement sections like verse and chorus.
A ducking effect where one sound (typically kick) reduces the volume of other sounds. Creates the classic pumping effect in electronic music. Added in OS 2.0.
An arrangement mode (OS 2.0) for chaining scenes into a complete song. Supports up to 99 song positions for compositions up to 9,801 bars long.
A playable slot within a group. Each group has 12 sounds (pads 0-9, +, -). A sound references a sample plus parameter settings like filter, envelope, and pitch.
A timing offset that delays every other note slightly, creating a shuffle or groove feel. Applied per-project and affects all groups equally.
Changing a sample's length without affecting its pitch. Useful for matching sample tempo to project tempo or creating special effects.
A sudden peak in audio signal, typically at the start of a drum hit or note. The EP-133's chop feature detects transients to automatically slice samples.
The function of the black Y knob, which is context-sensitive. In edit mode, it adjusts the selected parameter (filter, envelope, pitch, etc.).
